title: Ghodbunder Fort
tagline: Ghodbunder Fort — heritage city of Maharashtra

overview.short: Ghodbunder Fort is a fort located in Ghodbunder Village, Thane, Maharashtra, India, on the hill just south of the Ulhas River.
overview.description: Ghodbunder Fort is a fort located in Ghodbunder Village, Thane, Maharashtra, India, on the hill just south of the Ulhas River. It was built by the Portuguese and then occupied by the Marathas, before being used as the East India Company's district headquarters. The place was called Ghodbunder because it was where the Portuguese used to trade for ghode (horses) with the Arabs. Hence the name Ghodbunder: ghode (horses) + bunder (port).

topPlaces.name: Kashigaon metro station
topPlaces.description: Kashigaon is an elevated metro station on the Red Line of the Mumbai Metro, located in Kashigaon, Mira-Bhayandar, Maharashtra, India. The station is owned by the Mumbai Metropolitan Region Development Authority (MMRDA) and was opened to the public on 7 April 2026 as part of Phase 1 of Line 9.

topPlaces.name: Vasai Creek
topPlaces.description: Vasai Creek, previously Bassein Creek, is an estuary and one of the two main distributaries of the Ulhas River, in the Konkan division of Maharashtra, India. The Ulhas splits at the northeast corner of Salsette island into its two main distributaries, the other one being the Thane Creek, both of which empty into the Arabian Sea.

topPlaces.name: St. Jerome Church (Kashimira)
topPlaces.description: St. Jerome Church is a Roman Catholic Church in Kashimira, Mira-Bhayandar, India.

topPlaces.name: Kashigaon
topPlaces.description: Kashigaon is a town located on Ghodbunder Road in Maharashtra, India. It is now part of Thane.

topPlaces.name: Miragaon metro station
topPlaces.description: Miragaon is an elevated metro station on the Red Line of the Mumbai Metro, located in Kashimira, Mira-Bhayandar, Maharashtra, India. The station is owned by the Mumbai Metropolitan Region Development Authority (MMRDA) and was opened to the public on 7 April 2026 as part of Phase 1 of Line 9.

topPlaces.name: Umrao Hospitals
topPlaces.description: The Umrao Hospitals, Multi-speciality and Tertiary Care hospital is a private hospital located at the Asmita Enclave in the northern Mumbai suburb of Mira Road.

topPlaces.name: Mira Road
topPlaces.description: Mira Road (Marathi pronunciation: [miɾaː]) is a suburb north of Mumbai, in Thane District, and is part of the Mumbai Metropolitan Region. It falls within the jurisdiction of Mira-Bhayander, Vasai-Virar Police Commissionerate.

topPlaces.name: Mira-Bhayandar
topPlaces.description: Mira-Bhayandar is a city and municipal corporation in Thane district in the state of Maharashtra, India located in the northern part of Salsette Island before the Vasai Creek, and shares a border with North Mumbai. Mira-Bhayandar is administered by Mira-Bhayandar Municipal Corporation (MBMC), with a population at the 2011 Census of 809,378.

The station is owned by the Mumbai Metropolitan Region Development Authority (MMRDA) and was opened to the public on 7 April 2026 as part of Phase 1 of Line 9.

Vasai Creek, previously Bassein Creek, is an estuary and one of the two main distributaries of the Ulhas River, in the Konkan division of Maharashtra, India. The Ulhas splits at the northeast corner of Salsette island into its two main distributaries, the other one being the Thane Creek, both of which empty into the Arabian Sea.
St. Jerome Church is a Roman Catholic Church in Kashimira, Mira-Bhayandar, India.
Kashigaon is a town located on Ghodbunder Road in Maharashtra, India. It is now part of Thane.

Miragaon is an elevated metro station on the Red Line of the Mumbai Metro, located in Kashimira, Mira-Bhayandar, Maharashtra, India. The station is owned by the Mumbai Metropolitan Region Development Authority (MMRDA) and was opened to the public on 7 April 2026 as part of Phase 1 of Line 9.

Mira Road (Marathi pronunciation: [miɾaː]) is a suburb north of Mumbai, in Thane District, and is part of the Mumbai Metropolitan Region. It falls within the jurisdiction of Mira-Bhayander, Vasai-Virar Police Commissionerate.

Mira-Bhayandar is a city and municipal corporation in Thane district in the state of Maharashtra, India located in the northern part of Salsette Island before the Vasai Creek, and shares a border with North Mumbai. Mira-Bhayandar is administered by Mira-Bhayandar Municipal Corporation (MBMC), with a population at the 2011 Census of 809,378.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Ghodbunder Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Ghodbunder Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Ghodbunder Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport, Mumbai (23 km) and the nearest railway station is Kalyan Junction (Mumbai) (26 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Ghodbunder Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Ghodbunder Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Ghodbunder Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Ghodbunder Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Ghodbunder Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kashigaon metro station, Vasai Creek, St. Jerome Church (Kashimira), Kashigaon, Miragaon metro station.
